content.claim: The socioeconomic impact on mathematics performance of Hungarian 15-year-olds was among the largest in OECD countries, with high variance between schools, where selection is made based on students' records, and tracking can partially start at Grade 5 (age 10-11) or Grade 7 (age 12-13)
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Identifies tracking structure as a relevant feature but does not quantify the independent effect size of the 6/8-year track relative to other selection mechanisms
Data is from 2015 and does not reflect any subsequent policy changes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
